cards.prompt: The generated type of an async iterator implements IAsyncStateMachine; a plain sync iterator does not.
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Runtime.CompilerServices; using System.Threading.Tasks; static class D { public static IEnumerable<int> S(){ yield return 1; } public static async IAsyncEnumerable<int> A(){ await Task.Yield(); yield return 1; } } bool s = typeof(IAsyncStateMachine).IsAssignableFrom(D.S().GetType()); bool a = typeof(IAsyncStateMachine).IsAssignableFrom(D.A().GetType()); Console.WriteLine($"syncIterator.IAsyncStateMachine={s} asyncIterator.IAsyncStateMachine={a}");

cards.prompt: AsyncIteratorMethodBuilder is a struct with Create/MoveNext/Complete members (the builder for async iterators).
cards.code: using System; using System.Runtime.CompilerServices; var t = typeof(AsyncIteratorMethodBuilder); Console.WriteLine($"isStruct={t.IsValueType} Create={t.GetMethod("Create")!=null} MoveNext={t.GetMethod("MoveNext")!=null} Complete={t.GetMethod("Complete")!=null}");

cards.prompt: An async iterator interleaves await and yield return (yields 1 then 2) and its type is an async state machine.
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Runtime.CompilerServices; using System.Threading.Tasks; using System.Text; static class D { public static async IAsyncEnumerable<int> G(){ await Task.Yield(); yield return 1; await Task.Yield(); yield return 2; } } var sb = new StringBuilder(); await foreach(var x in D.G()) sb.Append(x); bool asyncSM = typeof(IAsyncStateMachine).IsAssignableFrom(D.G().GetType()); Console.WriteLine($"vals={sb} asyncStateMachine={asyncSM}");
